title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Israeli airstrikes kill 9 including Lebanese army officers after ceasefire deal
stories.summary:
Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon on Saturday killed nine people, including three high-ranking Lebanese military officers, according to the Lebanese army and state media. The attack occurred just days after Israel and Hezbollah agreed to a new ceasefire deal.

The Israeli military has launched an investigation into the incident, which targeted a vehicle carrying the soldiers. This development complicates regional efforts to stabilize the area following recent escalations involving Iran and its allies.

links.headline: Iran war live: Israel kills Lebanon general, Pakistan urges end to war
links.excerpt: Israeli forces kill three high-ranking Lebanese soldiers as Arab nations condemn Iran's attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait.
links.imageAlt: Iran war live: Israel kills Lebanon general, Pakistan urges end to war

stories.title: Disaster Declared as 2nd Screwworm Case Found in Texas near Border, Canada Bans Texas Cattle
stories.summary:
Texas has declared a state of disaster after confirming a second case of the New World screwworm, a flesh-eating parasite found in a calf near the Mexican border. The outbreak has prompted immediate international trade restrictions, with Canada banning all livestock imports from Texas.

Governor Greg Abbott described the situation as an imminent threat to public health and agriculture. The rapid spread of the infestation has raised concerns about containment efforts along the southern border.

links.headline: Canada bans Texas cattle over flesh-eating screwworm outbreak in US
links.excerpt: The ban came as a second infected calf was identified in Texas, and the US state declaring a state of disaster over the outbreak.
links.imageAlt: Canada bans Texas cattle over flesh-eating screwworm outbreak in US
links.alignmentBasis: source-default-fallback: no article alignment cues; source prior=Center
links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=high; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

links.headline: Disaster Declared as 2nd Screwworm Case Found in Texas near Border, Canada Bans Texas Cattle

stories.title: Cuba ex-President Raul Castro makes first appearance since U.S. charges to celebrate birthday
stories.summary:
Cuba ex-President Raul Castro makes first appearance since U.S. charges to celebrate birthday. Raul Castro, Cuba's low-profile former president and revolutionary guerilla, appeared in public for the first time since being indicted by the United States for his alleged role in the 1996 downing of two civilian aircraft, official video released Saturday showed.

Al Jazeera: Cuba’s Raul Castro makes first public appearance since US charges. Al Jazeera reports that former Cuban president Raul Castro made his first public appearance in Havana since being indicted by the United States.

stories.title: Armenia prepares for an election that could reshape ties with Moscow and the West
stories.summary:
Armenia prepares for an election that could reshape ties with Moscow and the West. Armenia's parliamentary elections Sunday will be a vote on its geopolitical future as incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan seeks closer relations with the European Union and the United States despite longstanding ties with Russia that have been championed by his critics.

stories.title: Newsroom edition: Labor broke a promise for bold reform. Does it matter? – Full Story podcast
stories.summary:
Newsroom edition: Labor broke a promise for bold reform. Does it matter? – Full Story podcast.

In a big gamble, Labor broke a big promise. In this week’s budget, the government announced changes to capital gains tax and negative gearing.

The treasurer, Jim Chalmers, is suggesting it’s the broken promise we had to have, in order to tackle intergenerational inequality and make the tax system fairer.

Reged Ahmad speaks with Mike Ticher, Patrick Keneally and Sarah Martin about when broken promises matter in politics <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/australia-news/2026/may. The Guardian: Newsroom edition: House prices in Australia are falling – will it hurt Labor? – Full Story podcast.

stories.title: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
stories.summary:
OpenAI has introduced a new feature called Lockdown Mode designed to safeguard sensitive information against prompt injection attacks. This update aims to minimize the risk of confidential data being inadvertently exposed through malicious prompts.

Despite these enhancements, ChatGPT may still remain susceptible to certain types of prompt injections. The primary objective is not complete immunity but rather a significant reduction in the probability that sensitive details are shared during such incidents.

links.headline: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
links.excerpt: Even with Lockdown Mode, ChatGPT could be still vulnerable to prompt injections, but the goal is to reduce the likelihood that sensitive data gets shared in the process.
links.imageAlt: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks

stories.title: ‘We should not have to sacrifice’: New York could become first state to temporarily ban large datacenters
stories.summary:
‘We should not have to sacrifice’: New York could become first state to temporarily ban large datacenters. Kristen Gonzalez, a state senator who authored the bill, said moratorium would target ‘hyperscale’ datacenters over 20MW New York moved closer toward becoming the first US state to enact a moratorium on large datacenters this week.

On Thursday, the state legislature approved a one-year ban on the facilities powering the AI boom. The measure now heads to Kathy Hochul, the governor, who will decide whether to sign it into law.

The Guardian spoke to a state senator in the wake of the.

stories.title: I asked AI for help with DIY. It told me to build a subfloor on rotting stumps, but also taught me valuable lessons | Myke Bartlett
stories.summary:
I asked AI for help with DIY. It told me to build a subfloor on rotting stumps, but also taught me valuable lessons | Myke Bartlett.

Nothing does more for your ego than realising you can make a better decision than a bot with all of human knowledge at its digital fingertips I am not, by nature, an early adopter. There comes a point in our lives where change becomes more irritating than exciting and, I suspect, I reached it sooner than most.

But when a workplace recently tasked me with exploring practical applications for AI, I spotted an opportunity to cast off my luddite inclinations. It turned out AI was very.

stories.title: Bukidnon tribal leaders reject NPA, reaffirm commitment to peace
stories.summary:
Bukidnon tribal leaders reject NPA, reaffirm commitment to peace.

IMPASUGONG, Bukidnon — Higaonon tribal leaders and Indigenous Peoples (IPs) from several Bukidnon towns formally declared their rejection of the Communist Party of the Philippines-New People’s Army-National Democratic Front (CPP-NPA-NDF) during a peace assembly held Thursday, June 4 in this town.

stories.title: Over 55,000 PNP personnel to be deployed for school opening
stories.summary:
Over 55,000 PNP personnel to be deployed for school opening.

MANILA, Philippines — As millions of students return to classrooms on Monday, the Philippine National Police (PNP) said it is “fully prepared and strategically positioned” to secure schools nationwide without compromising anti-crime and other public safety operations.

stories.title: Zamboanga del Sur sees agriculture growth with P345-M FMR projects
stories.summary: Zamboanga del Sur sees agriculture growth with P345-M FMR projects. PAGADIAN CITY, Zamboanga del Sur — The provincial government of Zamboanga del Sur has secured P345 million worth of farm-to-market (FMR) projects, a development seen to enhance agricultural productivity, ease transportation and stimulate economic growth in the area. Gov. Divina Grace Yu said the infrastructure investment will be funded by the Department of Agriculture (DA).

stories.title: My year with the robots: how Joanna Stern let AI into her home, work – and heart
stories.summary:
My year with the robots: how Joanna Stern let AI into her home, work – and heart. In 2025, the tech journalist invited artificial intelligence to do nearly everything for her, including editing the book she was writing about the experiment.

Some of it was useful, some not – but it was her time with a chatbot companion that really shook her For a year, Joanna Stern decided to turn herself into a “lab rat” – the object of her own experiment. Throughout 2025, she invited artificial intelligence into “every corner” of her life.
